gateway_mismatches.detail: User text contains XOR cue, but BPMN has no <choose mode='exclusive'>.
xor_to_seq => missing <choose mode='exclusive'>; treated as sequential (structural fallback)

user_text:
The process begins with the Mail Processing Unit collecting mail from the party. Once the mail is collected, a Mail Clerk sorts the unopened mail. After sorting, the Mail Clerk distributes the mail accordingly. The next step involves the Registry opening the mail. Once opened, the Registry sorts the mail and then registers it in a Mail Register.

Following the registration, the Assistant Registry Manager (ARM) performs a quality check on the mail. At this point, a decision is made. If the mail requires a list of requisitions, the ARM compiles this list and sends it back to the party. This concludes one possible path of the process.

Alternatively, if the mail involves matter details, the ARM captures these details and provides them to the Cashier. The Cashier then takes the applicable fees. After the fees are taken, two activities occur simultaneously. On one hand, the ARM puts the receipt and copies documents into an envelope, then posts these documents to the party. On the other hand, the Cashier captures the party details and prints the Physical Court File. Once both parallel activities are completed, the process paths converge.

Finally, the process concludes after either sending the list of requisitions back to the party or completing the steps involving the matter details and fees.
output_text:
The process begins with the Mail Processing Unit collecting mail from the party. Once the mail is collected, a Mail Clerk sorts the unopened mail. After sorting, the Mail Clerk distributes the mail accordingly.

Next, the Registry opens the mail and sorts it. Following this, the Registry registers the mail in a Mail Register. After registration, the Assistant Registry Manager (ARM) performs a quality check on the registered mail.

Once the quality check is complete, the ARM compiles a list of requisitions. This list is then sent back to the party by the ARM. After sending the list, the ARM captures the matter details related to the mail.

The ARM then provides these matter details to the Cashier. The Cashier takes the applicable fees from the party. At this point, the process splits into two parallel paths.

In the first path, the ARM puts the receipt into an envelope and copies the necessary documents into the same envelope. The ARM then posts these documents back to the party.

In the second parallel path, the Cashier captures the party details and prints the Physical Court File.

Both parallel paths conclude simultaneously, marking the end of the process.
